examples.title: Algorithm: MPI Timing Pattern with Barriers
examples.description: Demonstrates the standard pattern for accurate timing measurement in MIMD MPI programs using synchronization barriers
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the essential timing pattern for MPI programs. The key is using MPI_Barrier() before starting and stopping the timer to ensure all processes are synchronized. Without barriers, different processes might start/stop timing at different points, leading to inaccurate measurements. Only rank 0 needs to record times since all processes complete at the same barrier.

examples.title: Python: MPI Timing with mpi4py
examples.description: Python implementation demonstrating timing MPI parallel programs using mpi4py library
examples.code:
"""
MPI Timing Example in Python using mpi4py
Demonstrates accurate timing measurement for MIMD parallel programs
"""

from mpi4py import MPI
import numpy as np
import time

def parallel_computation(rank, size):
 """
 Simulate parallel work - each process computes independently
 Workload increases with rank to simulate load imbalance
 """
 # Simulate varying workloads across processes (load imbalance)
 work_items = 1000 + (rank * 100)
 result = 0
 for i in range(work_items):
 result += np.sqrt(i) * np.sin(i)
 return result

def main():
 # Initialize MPI
 comm = MPI.COMM_WORLD
 rank = comm.Get_rank()
 size = comm.Get_size()
 
 # Timing variables
 start_time = 0.0
 end_time = 0.0
 parallel_time = 0.0
 
 # Synchronize all processes before timing
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Only rank 0 starts the timer
 if rank == 0:
 start_time = MPI.Wtime()
 
 # === PARALLEL WORK SECTION ===
 # Each process performs its computation
 result = parallel_computation(rank, size)
 
 # Optional: Reduce results to rank 0 (simulates communication)
 total_result = comm.reduce(result, op=MPI.SUM, root=0)
 # ===============================
 
 # Synchronize all processes before stopping timer
 comm.Barrier()
 
 # Only rank 0 stops the timer and computes elapsed time
 if rank == 0:
 end_time = MPI.Wtime()
 parallel_time = end_time - start_time
 
 print(f"=== Timing Results ===")
 print(f"Number of processes: {size}")
 print(f"Total parallel time: {parallel_time:.6f} seconds")
 print(f"Total result: {total_result:.2f}")
 
 # Calculate estimated sequential time (for speedup)
 # In practice, run separately with size=1
 estimated_seq_time = parallel_time * size # Theoretical max
 speedup = estimated_seq_time / parallel_time
 efficiency = speedup / size * 100
 
 print(f"Estimated speedup: {speedup:.2f}x")
 print(f"Parallel efficiency: {efficiency:.1f}%")
 
 MPI.Finalize()

if __name__ == "__main__":
 main()

# To run: mpirun -n 4 python mpi_timing_example.py
examples.explanation: This Python implementation using mpi4py demonstrates the same timing pattern as C/MPI. Key points: 1) MPI.Wtime() provides high-resolution wall-clock time, 2) comm.Barrier() synchronizes all processes before timing, 3) Only rank 0 records times to avoid inconsistencies. The example includes speedup and efficiency calculations to show how timing data is used to evaluate parallel performance.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for parallel work, O(1) for timing overhead

examples.title: C: Complete MPI Timing Implementation
examples.description: Full C implementation showing accurate timing measurement for parallel MPI programs with performance metrics
examples.explanation: This complete C implementation demonstrates the standard timing pattern for MIMD MPI programs. The key methodology: 1) MPI_Barrier before timing ensures all processes have reached the starting point, 2) Only rank 0 calls MPI_Wtime to avoid inconsistent readings across processes, 3) Another barrier ensures all work is complete before stopping the timer, 4) The elapsed time is calculated as end_time - start_time. The example also shows calculation of speedup and parallel efficiency metrics.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for parallel computation, O(p) for barriers where p=number of processes
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for timing variables per process
